query: Write a python function to sort a list of integers.

metadata.search_query: Write a python function to sort a list of integers.

results.title: Sorting Techniques — Python 3.14.3 documentation
results.description: Pythonlistshave a built-inlist.sort() method that modifies thelistin-place. There is also a sorted() built-infunctionthat builds a new sortedlistfrom an iterable. In this document, we explore the various techniques for sorting data usingPython. Sorting Basics ¶ A simple ascendingsortis very easy: just call the sorted()function.

results.title: Python List sort () Method - GeeksforGeeks
results.description: Thesort() method is used to arrange the elements of alistin a specific order, either ascending or descending. It changes the originallistdirectly, so no newlistis created. This method works well for sorting numbers or strings. Let'ssortalistofnumbers in ascending order.

results.title: How to Use sorted() and .sort() in Python - Real Python
results.description: In this tutorial, you'll learn how tosortvarious types of data in different data structures inPython. You'll explore custom sorting orders and work with two distinct ways of sorting.

results.title: Python List sort () Method - W3Schools
results.description: Definition and Usage Thesort() methodsortsthelistascending by default. You can also make afunctiontodecide the sorting criteria (s).

results.title: sorting - Sort an integer list in Python - Stack Overflow
results.description: For that, something like How tosortalistofstrings numerically (2010),Sortalistofnumerical strings in ascending order (2012), and How tosortaPythonlistofstrings of numbers (2013). Though there must be a canonical from 2008 or 2009.
